findings.title: Recommended macOS backup selects every available category
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit recommended category list matching the product intent and prompt text, then use that list for "Backup recommended settings". Keep `macos_preference_category_ids` for the custom picker so users can opt into broader categories such as wallpaper, notifications, Spotlight/Siri, sharing, software update, services/extensions, and login items.

findings.title: duti export drops role-specific LaunchServices handlers
findings.suggested_fix: Export all supported LaunchServices role keys, not only `LSHandlerRoleAll`. For each handler, emit one duti rule for each present role key (`LSHandlerRoleAll` -> `all`, `LSHandlerRoleViewer` -> `viewer`, `LSHandlerRoleEditor` -> `editor`, `LSHandlerRoleShell` -> `shell`) paired with the content type or URL scheme, and add a regression fixture covering a handler that only has viewer/editor.

residual_risks: macOS defaults restore was validated through generation tests, but not by importing onto a clean macOS account.
The generated duti restore loop was not exercised against a real `duti` binary in this environment.
testing_gaps: No fixture asserts the exact category IDs chosen by the recommended macOS preferences path.
No LaunchServices fixture covers role-specific handlers without `LSHandlerRoleAll`.
`./tests/verify_fixes.sh` and `bash ./tests/generate_regressions.sh` passed; direct `./tests/generate_regressions.sh` failed because the file is not executable.
